source.title: Postoperative MRD status Refines Recurrence Risk Stratification Beyond Pathologic Response After Neoadjuvant Chemoradiotherapy for ESCC.

finding.description: MRD status significantly stratified recurrence-free survival and early recurrence risk, with 1-year recurrence rates of 6.1% vs 66.7% for MRD-negative vs MRD-positive groups.
finding.effect_summary: MRD status significantly stratified RFS with 1-year recurrence rates of 6.1% vs 66.7% for MRD-negative vs MRD-positive.

source_quote: MRD status significantly stratified RFS and early recurrence risk, with 1-year recurrence rates of 6.1% vs 66.7% for MRD-negative vs MRD-positive groups (P<.001).

source.title: Postoperative MRD status Refines Recurrence Risk Stratification Beyond Pathologic Response After Neoadjuvant Chemoradiotherapy for ESCC.

finding.description: Multivariable Cox-regression analysis demonstrated MRD positivity as an independent predictor of recurrence-free survival with a hazard ratio of 13.62.
finding.effect_summary: MRD positivity is an independent predictor of RFS with HR 13.62.

source_quote: Multivariable Cox-regression analysis demonstrated MRD positivity (hazard ratio, 13.62; P<.001) as an independent predictor of RFS, whereas pathologic response was not.

source.title: Postoperative MRD status Refines Recurrence Risk Stratification Beyond Pathologic Response After Neoadjuvant Chemoradiotherapy for ESCC.

finding.description: A classification combining pCR/non-pCR and MRD status identified four distinct recurrence risk subgroups with 1-year recurrence rates of 3.7% for pCR&MRD-negative, 7.7% for non-pCR&MRD-negative, 40% for pCR&MRD-positive, and 82.4% for non-pCR&MRD-positive.
finding.effect_summary: Four distinct recurrence risk subgroups identified with varying 1-year recurrence rates.

source_quote: A classification combining pCR/non-pCR and MRD status identified four distinct recurrence risk subgroups: the 1-year recurrence rates were 3.7% of pCR&MRD-negative, 7.7% of non-pCR&MRD-negative, 40% of pCR&MRD-positive, and 82.4% of non-pCR&MRD-positive (P<.001), with distant recurrence predominating in MRD-positive groups.
